channel.title: article on Analyst
channel.description: Recent content in article on Analyst

item.title: General
item.description: This article is about some general stuff that happens on the physical layer. CSMA/CD =

item.title: TCP/IP Stack Overview
item.description: This article is an overview of TCP/IP protocol stack. I will cover in detail what happens when a request to, say, bakerst221b.

item.title: Forensic Analysis Plan
item.description: Intro Main idea is to concentrate on some areas, answer some specific questions, like: Is there child exploitation material?

item.title: Apple Encryption
item.description: Applications or even OS itself doesn’t know the keys that are protected by Secure Enclave. OS only sees Keychain, but the Security Enclave is separated.

item.title: Attacks
item.description: ​ TCP session hijacking

item.title: Apple Mach-O File
item.description: Intro Using otool we can get general info about the Mach-O file: otool -h [app_name] > Mach header magic cputype cpusubtype caps filetype ncmds sizeofcmds flags 0xfeedfacf 16777228 0 0x00 2 115 11384 0x00218085 Quick refernce on the meaning:

item.title: MacOS Fundamentals
item.description: Firewall You can set specific rules to limit the traffic. FireVault No recovery once this password is lost.

item.title: Devices
item.description: This article is about devices that reside on the physical layer of OSI model. Hubs and Repeaters Since the max length of a cable (10BASE5 thick Ethernet) that connects two machines cannot exceed 500 m (according to the specs), there is a need to amplify and control the signal.

item.title: Incident Detection
item.description: http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/security-articles.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/network-protocol-analyzers/1224-performing-tcp-syn-flood-attack-and-detecting-it-with-wireshark.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/network-protocol-analyzers/1222-detecting-syn-flood-attacks-capsa-network-protocol-analyzer.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/network-protocol-analyzers/1139-nchronos-brute-force-attack-detection.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/network-protocol-analyzers/1119-capsa-network-analyzer-detect-p2p-file-sharing-torrent-traffic.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/network-protocol-analyzers/1113-capsa-network-analyzer-discover-arp-attacks-flooding.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/network-protocol-analyzers/1102-how-to-detect-routing-and-physical-loops-using-a-network-analyzer.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/book-reviews/187-network-security-titles/1020-tao-network-security-monitoring.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/security-articles/288-ids-info.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/e-mail-security-white-papers/279-email-security-exploit-detection.html http://www.firewall.cx/security-news/273-news-catch-hackers.html http://www.firewall.cx/education-news/793-extrusion-detection-security-monitoring-for-internal-intrusions.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/security-articles/272-news-log-based-id-gfi.html http://www.firewall.cx/networking-topics/protocols/netflow/1236-manageengine-netflow-analyzer.html http://www.firewall.cx/general-topics-reviews/web-application-vulnerability-scanners/1199-protecting-user-cookies-from-xss-vulnerabilities-attacks.html

item.title: First Response
item.description: Anatomy of an investigation: Identification/Assessment. Understand your scope (technical and legal aspects), your goals and people you are going to work with.

item.title: First Response
item.description: Anatomy of an investigation: Identification/Assessment. Understand your scope (technical and legal aspects), your goals and people you are going to work with.

item.title: IBM X-Force Cyberattack Framework
item.description: As usual, there is a table that can be filtered in order to find what’s most suitable here.

item.title: Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description: Host-based IDS look for local system configurations and behaviour, while network-based IDS look for network connections and looks for anomalies and common signatures.

item.title: Network Triage
item.description: Intro Ports idetify services (usually, but not always). System ports are forensically important 0-1023. User ports 1024-49151 Private 49152-last Cheatsheets for known ports here.

item.title: Hiding data
item.description: There lots of ways to hide data. ADS Alternate data streams. Specific for Windows. See more here.

item.title: Hiding data
item.description: There lots of ways to hide data. ADS Alternate data streams. Specific for Windows. See more here.

item.title: Mobile Forensic Analysis Introduction
item.description: Tool leveling pyramid The methods are placed in the order from the easiest to the hardest and most time-consuming:

item.title: Malware Analysis Tips
item.description: In this article I’m collecting all the main tips for malware analysis. Some indicators of compromise, some common clues and etc.

item.title: Distributed Systems Security
item.description: This one was written during my preparation to the GCFA exam and is also essential for my CISSP certification preparation.

item.title: Phishing
item.description: Phishing, aka brand spoofing, aka carding - various scam that primarily use e-mail messages to trick a person into divulging personal information.

item.title: Point of Sale Breack
item.description: References

item.title: Threat Hunting And Intelligence
item.description: Threat Intelligence - ??? Security Intelligence - The real-time collection, normalization, and analysis of the data generated by users, applications, and infrastructure that impacts the IT security and risk posture of an enterprise

item.title: Threat Landscape
item.description: 2020 Ransomware attacks are becoming more common. The amout of scanning and CVE-2019-19781: Citrix Application Delivery Controller.

item.title: Attacks
item.description: This is a protocol of TCP/IP suite, Data Link Layer. The first layer after physical layer is a data link layer and the protocol in use here is Ethernet II.

item.title: Attacks
item.description: This is a protocol of TCP/IP suite, Data Link Layer. The first layer after physical layer is a data link layer and the protocol in use here is Ethernet II.

item.title: Ethernet
item.description: This is a protocol of TCP/IP suite, Data Link Layer. The first layer after physical layer is a data link layer and the protocol in use here is Ethernet II.

item.title: 3D Secure
item.description: This article is about 3DS protocol (XML-based) which is used to secure online payments. There are three parties in this process: merchant, aquirer and the bank (the issuer of the card).

item.title: DHCP
item.description: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol. Listening on 67 port. Sending from 68. DHCP Discover (Broadcast) C-> S

item.title: DNS
item.description: Domain Name System. Translates IPs to domain names like 192.168.1.1 to router.lan and visa versa.

item.title: Kerberos
item.description: Intro Secure shell. Significant improvement over telnet for remote control (since telnet was not encrypting the connection).

item.title: SMB
item.description: Over TCP - 445 port Over NetBIOS API UDP - ports 137, 138 TCP - ports 137, 139 NBF (legacy) Powershell command + win reg for SMB 1,2,3.

item.title: SSH
item.description: Intro Secure shell. Common ports: 22. Structure ![photo_2021-02-16 11.58.08](images/photo_2021-02-16 11.58.08.jpeg) The Flow - Wanna talk

item.title: Syslog
item.description: Not sure yet wether it belongs to this section. It makes the most sense to put it here until I am sure.

item.description: Lorem markdownum aequalis strigis. Saetigeri iubeas, vultu huic alvum nondum de obside ut laniavit arbor palmis, cum quin.

item.title: SSL and TLS
item.description: This article explains how SSL and its successsor work. Intro SSL makes use of assymmetric encryption for verification and symmetric encryption for data exchanging.

item.title: Diffie Hellman
item.description: I’ve written this article while I was untanging RSA and Deffie Hellman inside my head. What I need to stree right here, in the article description, the main difference between them is the Deffie Hellman is NOT a encryption algorithm!

item.title: Randomness
item.description: In this article I am assembling information about randomness in computer science: how it is implemented, used and how it can be attacked.

item.title: RSA
item.description: To my utter embarrassment I had to admit that I’ve recently realized I didn’t remember how the RSA worked….

item.title: File Systems
item.description: Partitioning is the address range where the volume with a file system can sit. You create a partition, then you format it to create a volume with a file system.

item.title: Compilation and Linking
item.description: In this article I am trying to reasearch compilation and linking process. Terms .cpp - is a human-readable file written in any programming language.

item.title: Interrupts
item.description: This is an article about hardware interrupts, how they are designed and what for are used.

item.title: Positive and Negative Numbers
item.description: Intro One’s complement Two complement

item.title: Processes, threads and mutants
item.description: In this article I am describing the difference between a process, thread and mutex. Process When and exe file or other executable file is run, some memory in RAM is allocated and the program’s code and data is read from the hard drive and written into RAM.

item.title: Constitutional Implications
item.description: First Amendment Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; or abridging the freedom of speech, or of the press; or the right of the people peaceably to assemble, and to petition the Government for a redress of grievances.

item.title: Linux Artifacts
item.description: References

item.title: Privacy and Admissability of Evidence
item.description: There is some information that is considered privileged. This can be excluded from the legal search even if there is a warrant.

item.title: Timelines
item.description: If something was downloaded and the accessed in several days - then the suspect knew about this file.

item.title: Mutable vs Immutable
item.description: >>> import ctypes >>> a = 5 >>> address = id(a) >>> address 4307917216 >>> ctypes.

item.title: Account Information
item.description: Password hashes MacOS sudo plutil -p /var/db/dslocal/nodes/Default/users/<usename>.plist To crack: Windows SAM hive Linux etc/passwd and etc/shadow.

item.title: Frida Crash Course
item.description: Intro Frida is cool Templates Empty If a method needs an argument of type Context, getApplicationContext() needs to be used.

item.title: Mac Artifacts
item.description: Todo https://docviewer.yandex.ru/view/0/?page=2&*%3D&lang=en https://blog.elcomsoft.com/2016/07/mac-os-forensics-attacking-filevault-2/ https://macforensicslab.com/2011/01/01/general-forensics-tips-mac-platform/ https://forensicswiki.xyz/wiki/index.php?title=Mac_OS_X https://github.com/pstirparo/mac4n6 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DgiljzWXkzc&t=21s https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5VKTaFBlMcE https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FOpiDSAD-Yk https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/.DS_Store OSDF Conference 2020 - Apollo

item.title: IOS Screen Time
item.description: Overview Address book 📖 Call history ☎️ SMS 💬 MMS 💬 🌄 Email ✉️ Web browser history Photos 📷 Videos 📹 Music 🎵 Documents 📃 Calendar 📆 Network communication 🌐 Maps 🗺️ Social networking 🦜 Deleted data 🗑️ https://www.

item.title: Ios Decrypting Applications Overview
item.description: There are several tools available that perform decryption of iOS applications. iOS applications that are downloaded from AppStore are encrypted.

item.title: Ios Decrypting Applications Overview
item.description: There are several tools available that perform decryption of iOS applications. iOS applications that are downloaded from AppStore are encrypted.

item.title: Evasion Techniques
item.description: Evasion techniques and what possible forensic footprints they are likely to leave? VPN Virtual private network.

item.title: Evasion Techniques
item.description: VPN Virtual private network. As an additional bonus - avoid bandwidth throttling (when ISP cuts your speed or bandwidth for certain resources like torrents).

item.title: Proxying Traffic on iOS
item.description: There might be several reasons to intercept traffic: just sniffing or also modifying it. In this article I’m going to describe basic techniques of intercepting traffic coming on and from iOS devices.

item.title: RATs
item.description: This is not about rodents and pest controls. This is about remote control. Intro 🐀 RAT - a tool that allows remote control of another machine.

item.title: Rootkits
item.description: Rootkits are provide the user full system access and operate on the kernel level thus unvisible for many security tools.

item.title: Staying Persistent
item.description: To stay persistent, it’s neccesary to stay quite, at least for some time. Rootkits Registry

item.title: HDD Overview
item.description: In this article I’m going to give a general overview of how HDDs work. Floppy Floppy disks 💾 are like dinosaurs 🦕.

item.title: Antiforensics
item.description: There are several ways to hide day. Some are specific to the OS, some are general.

item.title: Local Authentication
item.description: This article is all about such scary terms as LSA, local authentication, SAM etc. Also, I’ve tries to highlight the updates on the implementation for different Windows versions.

item.title: Alloc8
item.description: So, there are some prerequisites that one needs to have in order to get this exploit.

item.title: Calling Conventions in x86
item.description: In this article I’m giving an overview of different calling conventions with examples. Intro Consider the following code snippet:

item.title: Flags
item.description: CF ZF OF

item.title: Key Terms
item.description: Information security means protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction in order to provide: A.

item.title: Operations
item.description: In this article I’m describing all assembly operations that I’ve encountered myseld and also wasn’t lazy anough to put down an explanation about here.

item.title: Registers
item.description: EAX EIP EBP RSI/ESI Source Index. RSI - 64 bit, ESI - 32. RDI/EDI Destination Index.

item.title: C Program Memory Layout
item.description: Intro At the very beginning of my career I found it hard to grasp the notion of memory layout and stack.

item.title: Crackme Algorithm
item.description: Look at the strings fs strings and f in radare2 or rabin -zz Find xrefs for the strings of interest axt @ str.

item.title: ARM Assembly
item.description: In this article I’m giving a quick dive-in into assembly. It’s very simple and very difficult at the same time.

item.title: Assembly Intro
item.description: In this article I’m giving a quick dive-in into assembly. It’s very simple and very difficult at the same time.

item.title: ARP
item.description: This article is about ARP protocol. It’s used to map IP addresses to physical (MAC) addresses.

item.title: Attacks
item.description: MAC flooding Span port Everytime you receive messages fir these ports (say 1 to 10), also send them to the span port 25.

item.title: Devices
item.description: This article is about the network devices that operate on the Data Link layer. These devices know.

item.title: IP
item.description: *This article is about IP protocol (v4, v6 and IPX).

item.title: Live Acquisition Steps
item.description: Any activity on a live-system is changing the state of it. About 96%, though, remains unchanged.

item.title: Mac Imaging
item.description: I’ve recently listened to DFSP podcast in which Steve Wahlen talked about different caveats in Mac forensics.

item.title: Mobile Acquisition Methods
item.description: In this article I’m studying different acquisition methods, tool available for each, pros and cons of each tool as well.

item.title: Mobile Device Characteristics
item.description: Mobile devices consist of the following parts: Microprocessor ROM (read-only memory) RAM (random access memory) - execution of code happens here.

item.title: Offline Acquisition Steps
item.description: Prerequisites Live USB/CD? Process Connect external HDD or SSD Lauch some forensic distribution (for example, Paladin).

item.title: USBKill Switch
item.description: A software that could be used for anti-forensics. By default, configs are at /etc and named usbkill.

item.title: Vehicle Forensics
item.description: CMD42 Lock ATA locks on HDDs. Usually 2.5'' HDD, verify there is a lock with MHDD, PC 3000.

item.title: iOS Introduction
item.description: Low Level Security UID - key that is fused into Application processor. No one know it and can get from the device.

item.title: Android Applications Intro
item.description: Android Overview I’m not going to describe in all the details the basics of this architecture for it’s described pretty good here: Mobile Security Guide link.

item.title: Android Booting Process
item.description: This article describes Android booting process. Booting Process Step 1: Power On and System Startup. When we press the power button, the Boot ROM code starts executing from a pre-defined location which is hardwired in ROM.

item.title: iOS Booting Process
item.description: When an iDevice is started, two separate booting processes are taking place: booting of Application Processor and booting of Secure Enclave.

item.title: iOS Structure
item.description: When installed on the device, *.ipa is unzipped into a corresponding folder. That’s the contents:

item.title: Apple File Systems
item.description: HFS HFS+ Supports large files Efficient disk space usage unicode for filenames name forks file compression journaling dynamic resizing, defragmentation not only for MacOS APFS Clones Snapshots Space sharing encryption crash protection sparse files fast directory resizing iOS has two partitions: system and user.

item.title: iDevice Internals
item.description: This article is about device internals for different versions of iDevices. This can be helpful for forensic examiners since the security mechanisms are sometimes quite different.

item.title: ToDO
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bmJSWrVuIVQ https://mobile-security.gitbook.io/mobile-security-testing-guide/ios-testing-guide/0x06h-testing-platform-interaction cycript #todo*: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/network/implementing_netcat_with_network_framework

item.title: iOS Jailbreaking
item.description: Types of jailbreaking Tethered jailbreaks don’t persist through reboots, so re-applying jailbreaks requires the device to be connected (tethered) to a computer during every reboot.

item.title: iOS Keychain
item.description: In this article I’m trying to study how keychain works. Metaphor There once lived a monkey 🐒 George.

item.title: Windows Registry
item.description: The registry is a dynamically expanding database of binary and ASCII data. Its primary purpose is to store configurations.
